title: Define a raw register-user DTO
scenario: An HTTP adapter receives partial, untrusted data. Start by representing that boundary data separately from the domain command.
instructions: Define RegisterUserDto with public email: Option<String> and display_name: Option<String> fields.

hints.body: Model raw adapter input as a plain DTO. Use Option<String> because either field may be absent at the boundary.
Keep DTO fields public; this type represents input data, not validated domain state.
One complete solution for Define a raw register-user DTO is shown below.

completionExplanation: The raw DTO can represent partial boundary input without pretending it is already valid domain data.
